message:
fix(gateway): Correct pagination cursors and root path detection

Fixes two logic bugs in the gateway services:

Pagination Cursor Fix (reservation.service.ts):
- listReservations() and listConflicts() were generating prevCursor
  even on the first page (when startIndex === 0)
- Added condition: only generate prevCursor when startIndex > 0
- Fixes issue where first page would show a "previous" link that
  returned empty results

Safety Rule Fix (safety-rules.engine.ts):
- The "block root recursive delete" rule used glob pattern "/**"
  which matches ANY recursive delete path, not just root
- Changed to exact pattern "/" to only block operations where
  path exactly equals the filesystem root
- Prevents false positives on legitimate recursive operations

Both fixes prevent confusing UX behavior and overly broad security
checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -388,7 +388,7 @@ export function getDefaultRules(): SafetyRule[] {
       category: "filesystem",
       conditions: [
         { field: "operation", patternType: "exact", pattern: "delete" },
-        { field: "path", patternType: "glob", pattern: "/**" },
+        { field: "path", patternType: "exact", pattern: "/" },
         { field: "recursive", patternType: "exact", pattern: "true" },
       ],
       conditionLogic: "and",
